taxTypes.label: Employment and PAYE
taxTypes.scope: UK employment Income Tax rates, statutory mileage amounts and benefits in kind under ITEPA 2003. PAYE is the collection system; separate National Insurance treatment can also apply.

sections.heading: Approved mileage allowance payments
rows: Qualifying fellow-employee passenger in a car or van

sections.heading: Vehicle benefits and official interest
rows: No charge may arise where an exemption or restricted private-use rule applies.
Multiply by the company car’s appropriate percentage; private fuel rules apply.
Applies where taxable private fuel is provided.
Used for certain employment-related loans and accommodation; reviewed quarterly and may change during the tax year.

sections.heading: Statutory payments
rows: £123.25 weekly or 80% of average weekly earnings, whichever is lower
£194.32 or 90% of average weekly earnings, whichever is lower
Payable for up to the statutory period if eligible.
Paternity, Shared Parental, Parental Bereavement and Neonatal Care Pay
£194.32 or 90% of average weekly earnings, whichever is lower
Separate eligibility and duration rules apply.

sections.heading: Legacy employer-supported childcare
rows: Closed to new applicants from 4 October 2018; qualifying existing members may continue.
Higher-rate taxpayer who joined on or after 6 April 2011
Employer’s basic earnings assessment and continuing eligibility rules apply.
Additional-rate taxpayer who joined on or after 6 April 2011
Employer’s basic earnings assessment and continuing eligibility rules apply.

rows: Qualifying fellow-employee passenger in a car or van

rows: Other percentages depend on CO₂ emissions and, for certain cars, electric range.
Exemptions and restricted private-use rules may apply.
Multiply by the company car’s appropriate percentage.

sections.heading: Selected statutory payments
rows: £187.18 or 90% of average weekly earnings, whichever was lower
Separate first-six-week and eligibility rules applied where relevant.

sections.heading: Legacy employer-supported childcare
rows: Closed to new applicants from 4 October 2018; qualifying existing members could continue.

taxTypes.label: Capital Gains Tax (CGT)
taxTypes.scope: UK Capital Gains Tax. The individual rates depend on how taxable gains sit above taxable income in the Income Tax bands.

sections.heading: Main rates and annual exempt amounts
rows: Individuals — gains within unused basic-rate band
The balance above that band is normally charged at 24%.
Taxable income is considered before taxable gains.
Subject to the rules for the particular trust or estate.
Business Asset Disposal Relief or Investors’ Relief
Annual exempt amount — individuals and personal representatives
Deducted from gains, subject to eligibility.
Special sharing rules can apply to related trusts.

sections.heading: How to use the short-lease table
rows: A non-renewable lease of 50 years or less is generally treated as a wasting asset.
The calculation depends on the transaction and the relevant lease rules.
Use the complete-year percentage plus one twelfth of the difference to the next percentage for each additional month.
Count 14 days or more as one month.
The percentages are calculation factors, not rates of Capital Gains Tax.
Review TCGA 1992 and HMRC’s lease guidance before applying them.

sections.heading: Main rates and annual exempt amounts
rows: Individuals — gains within unused basic-rate band
The balance above that band is normally charged at 24%.
Taxable income is considered before taxable gains.
Business Asset Disposal Relief or Investors’ Relief
Annual exempt amount — individuals and personal representatives

sections.heading: Historical rates, exemptions and lifetime limits
rows: Individuals — gains within unused basic-rate band
Residential-property gains were normally charged at 18%.
Individuals — gains above the basic-rate band
Residential-property gains were normally charged at 28%.
Residential-property gains were normally charged at 28%.
The lifetime limit on qualifying gains was £1 million.
The lifetime limit on qualifying gains was £10 million; this is not the current limit.
Annual exempt amount — individuals and personal representatives
Special sharing rules could apply to related trusts.

taxTypes.label: National Insurance
taxTypes.scope: UK National Insurance contributions. Employment Income Tax, mileage allowances and benefits in kind are shown separately under Employment and PAYE.

taxTypes.label: Pensions tax limits
taxTypes.scope: UK registered-pension tax limits. These are allowances and limits, not rates charged directly to every contribution or withdrawal.

sections.heading: Main pension allowances and limits
rows: Unused allowance may be carried forward subject to conditions.
Can apply after flexible access to money-purchase benefits.
The taper applies only if the adjusted-income test is also met.
Allowance reduces by £1 for each £2 above this amount.
The taper cannot reduce the allowance below this amount.
Individual protections and transitional rules can change the result.
Lump sum and death benefit allowance
Individual protections and exclusions may apply.

taxTypes.label: Stamp Duty Land Tax (SDLT)
taxTypes.scope: England and Northern Ireland only. Scotland uses Land and Buildings Transaction Tax and Wales uses Land Transaction Tax.

periods.label: From 1 April 2025
periods.effective: Transactions with an effective date on or after 1 April 2025

sections.heading: Standard residential rates
rows: Higher rates or the non-resident surcharge may apply.

sections.heading: Higher rates for additional dwellings
rows: Detailed ownership, replacement-home and refund rules apply.

sections.heading: Non-residential or mixed property
rows: Lease rent uses separate net-present-value rates.

taxTypes.label: Foreign Income and Gains (FIG)
taxTypes.scope: Selected special rates within the post-6 April 2025 Foreign Income and Gains regime. Ordinary taxable FIG uses the relevant Income Tax or Capital Gains Tax rates.

sections.heading: Temporary Repatriation Facility
rows: Qualifying pre-6 April 2025 foreign income and gains
A valid designation and all statutory conditions are required.

sections.heading: Temporary Repatriation Facility
rows: Qualifying pre-6 April 2025 foreign income and gains
A valid designation and all statutory conditions are required.

taxTypes.label: Trust tax
taxTypes.scope: UK trustee Income Tax and selected trust limits. The settlor, beneficiary, residence and trust type can change who is taxable.

sections.heading: Trustee Income Tax
rows: Trustees do not receive the dividend allowance.
Accumulation or discretionary trust — other income
Subject to the trust tax-free amount rule.
Beneficiary and tax-credit rules also need consideration.
Interest in possession trust — other income
Mandated income may instead be taxed directly on the beneficiary.
If income exceeds the limit, tax is normally due on the full amount; the limit can be divided between trusts.

sections.heading: Trustee Income Tax
rows: Trustees do not receive the dividend allowance.
Accumulation or discretionary trust — other income
Subject to the trust tax-free amount rule.
Beneficiary and tax-credit rules also need consideration.
Interest in possession trust — other income
Mandated income may instead be taxed directly on the beneficiary.
The limit can be divided between trusts.

taxTypes.label: Corporation Tax (CT)
taxTypes.scope: UK Corporation Tax rates and selected relief rates. Corporation Tax uses financial years beginning 1 April; a company accounting period can span two financial years. The detailed Corporation Tax study topic is planned under BTAX/CTAX/.

sections.heading: Corporation Tax rates and marginal relief
rows: Generally applies where augmented profits exceed the upper limit.
Generally applies where augmented profits do not exceed the lower limit; some companies, including close investment-holding companies, cannot use it.
The limits are reduced for short accounting periods and divided by the number of associated companies.
Marginal Relief may apply between the adjusted lower and upper limits.
The effective marginal rate is 26.5% in the standard £50,000 to £250,000 range before adjusting the limits.
Applies to qualifying Patent Box profits where the statutory conditions and election requirements are met.

sections.heading: Research and Development relief
rows: For accounting periods beginning on or after 1 April 2024. The taxable above-the-line credit is subject to qualifying-cost, claim, notification and PAYE-cap rules.
86% additional deduction; payable credit normally 14.5% of the surrenderable loss
For qualifying loss-making R&D-intensive small or medium-sized enterprises. Detailed intensity, loss, expenditure, claim and PAYE-cap conditions apply.

sections.heading: Chargeable gains indexation reference
rows: Frozen at the Retail Prices Index for December 2017

For a qualifying asset acquired before 1 January 2018, indexation may reflect inflation only up to December 2017. Use HMRC’s official indexation-factor tables rather than the raw Retail Prices Index table.

sections.heading: Corporation Tax and selected historical reliefs
rows: Applied to non-ring-fence profits in financial year 2022.
Subject to the Patent Box conditions and election requirements.
Historical scheme. The exact treatment depends on when the accounting period began and when expenditure was incurred.
Historical above-the-line credit. Later rates and the post-1 April 2024 schemes are different.

taxTypes.label: Inheritance Tax (IHT)
taxTypes.scope: UK Inheritance Tax rates, thresholds and selected reliefs. Liability can depend on residence history, the type and timing of a transfer, exemptions, ownership, valuation and detailed relief conditions. The detailed IHT study topic is planned under IHT/.

sections.heading: Main rates and thresholds
rows: Normally applies to the chargeable estate above the available nil-rate bands after exemptions and reliefs.
May apply where at least 10% of the relevant net estate component is left to charity; detailed component rules apply.
The lifetime rate for immediately chargeable transfers; further tax can arise if the transferor dies within seven years.
Unused percentage may be transferable from a deceased spouse or civil partner, subject to a claim and conditions.
For a qualifying residence closely inherited by direct descendants; limited by the qualifying residential interest and other conditions.
The taper uses the value of the estate before exemptions and reliefs; the residence nil-rate band can be reduced to nil.

sections.heading: Agricultural and Business Relief from 6 April 2026
rows: Shared across qualifying agricultural and business property. Any unused allowance may be transferable from a deceased spouse or civil partner, potentially increasing it to £5 million.
The allowance is apportioned across qualifying property; detailed ownership, activity, holding-period, trust and anti-avoidance rules apply.
Shares admitted to trading but not listed
Includes qualifying shares traded on markets such as AIM; no part receives the 100% allowance under this category.

sections.heading: Lifetime gift exemptions
rows: Unused exemption may be carried forward for one tax year only; the current year’s exemption is used first.
Up to £250 per recipient each tax year
May be used for any number of recipients, but not for someone who receives another exemption from the same donor in that tax year.
Must be made in consideration of the marriage or civil partnership; statutory conditions apply.
Wedding or civil-partnership gift — grandchild or great-grandchild
The relationship is between the donor and the person marrying or entering the civil partnership.
Wedding or civil-partnership gift — any other person
May be combined with the annual exemption, but not the small-gift exemption for the same recipient.
Must form part of normal expenditure, be made from income and leave enough income to maintain the donor’s usual standard of living.

sections.heading: Relief linked to timing
rows: Taper relief on tax attributable to a lifetime gift
Taper relief on tax attributable to a lifetime gift
Taper relief on tax attributable to a lifetime gift
Taper relief on tax attributable to a lifetime gift
Taper relief on tax attributable to a lifetime gift
More than 1 but not more than 2 years
More than 2 but not more than 3 years
More than 3 but not more than 4 years
More than 4 but not more than 5 years

sections.heading: Main historical rates and thresholds
rows: Applied to the chargeable estate above the available nil-rate bands after exemptions and reliefs.
Could apply where the charitable-giving conditions were met.
Applied to immediately chargeable lifetime transfers, subject to the detailed rules.
Qualifying-home, direct-descendant and taper rules applied.
The band reduced by £1 for every £2 above the threshold.

sections.heading: Historical relief note
rows: Existing qualifying property could receive 100% or 50% relief
The £2.5 million combined cap did not apply in 2022/23; it applies from 6 April 2026.

sections.heading: Historical lifetime gift exemptions
rows: Unused exemption could be carried forward for one tax year only.
The normality, income and standard-of-living conditions applied.

taxTypes.label: Tax administration
